title: Vignea conjugata
taxonomy: Vignea conjugata is classified under the family Convolvulaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the morning glory family. This family consists of around 1,800 species, including Vignea conjugata. The Convolvulaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Vignea conjugata belongs to the subfamily Convolvuloideae. Vignea conjugata is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Its classification also includes being a member of the order Solanales and the class Magnoliopsida.

constituents: Vignea conjugata contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These bioactive compounds contribute to the plant's potential health benefits. The flavonoids present in Vignea conjugata may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Saponins have been reported to possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Additionally, the plant's phenolic acids may have cardioprotective effects.
